skill.name: design-web
skill.description: Use @partner-up-dev/design-web Vue components correctly in application UI.

forms.label: Forms
forms.intent: Inputs, selection, upload, and form structure.

display.label: Display
display.intent: Content presentation, status, navigation, and compact structure.

layout.label: Layout
layout.intent: Page scaffolds, scroll regions, and structural containers.

skillReferences.description: durable package principles for component ownership, composition, tokens, and local CSS.
durable package rules for public imports, TypeScript helper types, and Vue global component declarations.
durable package vocabulary for tone, status tone, control variants, container variants, field variants, surface levels, and shapes.
package-local visual direction for semantic color, quiet typography, and square-corner rectangular defaults.

preferWhen: Use for expandable content sections where users reveal or hide details.
avoidWhen: Avoid for navigation tabs or mutually exclusive view switching.

preferWhen: Use inside PuAccordion for each expandable panel.
avoidWhen: Do not use outside PuAccordion unless the component explicitly supports standalone usage.

preferWhen: Use for overview surfaces where tiles have different emphasis and spans.
avoidWhen: Avoid for simple equal-column lists; use normal layout or cards instead.

preferWhen: Use inside PuBentoGrid for an individual overview tile.
avoidWhen: Avoid as a general-purpose card outside bento layouts.
apiCaveats: Use surfaceLevel for neutral page/content depth and variant for plain, soft, outline, or solid treatment.

preferWhen: Use for user-triggered commands, form actions, compact icon commands, link-styled calls to action, and transient pending/success/error feedback actions.
avoidWhen: Avoid for plain inline text links.
apiCaveats: Use the structured action prop with the shared PuAction type for native, href, or route targets; route actions render the consuming app's globally registered RouterLink.
Use tone for semantic color intent and variant for visual treatment such as solid, soft, outline, ghost, or dashed.
Use feedback for transient idle, pending, success, and error action states.
accessibilityNotes: Icon-only buttons need an accessible label.
Disabled link actions use aria-disabled and stop click navigation.

preferWhen: Use for grouped content with optional hero, header, body, footer, collapsible content, or whole-card action targets.
avoidWhen: Avoid for dense list rows; use PuCellGroup and PuCell for row-based settings or lists.
Avoid putting buttons, links, or form controls inside an interactive card root.
apiCaveats: Use the structured action prop with the shared PuAction type for href or route card targets; route actions render the consuming app's globally registered RouterLink.
Use tone for semantic emphasis and variant for container treatment such as soft, outline, plain, or solid.
Use padding with the shared PuSpacing vocabulary when card insets need to differ from the default.

Use selectable plus active for button-like choice cards without a navigation target; use usePuSelect when a card option set should share selection behavior without coupling it to a card-specific component.
Use keepContentMounted only when collapsed content owns local state that must survive collapse.
accessibilityNotes: Interactive cards must not contain nested interactive controls.
Disabled link and route cards use aria-disabled and stop click navigation.

preferWhen: Use for a compact row with label, description, meta, icon, action, or navigation affordance.
avoidWhen: Avoid for grouped facts that should use PuDescriptionList.
apiCaveats: Use padding with the shared PuSpacing vocabulary for row inset control; min-height remains a separate row rhythm decision.

preferWhen: Use to group PuCell rows into a titled or visually bounded section.
avoidWhen: Avoid for free-form card content; use PuCard.
apiCaveats: Use surfaceLevel for neutral page/content depth and variant for plain, soft, outline, or solid treatment.

preferWhen: Use for an individual boolean choice or confirmation field.
avoidWhen: Avoid for exclusive choices; use PuTabs or PuPicker depending on context.
apiCaveats: Use boolean modelValue as the value contract.
Use size sm, md, or lg from PuSize.
Use tone neutral, primary, secondary, tertiary, or danger from PuTone.
The component renders native checkbox input semantics and a square token-driven visual box.

preferWhen: Use for compact labels, filters, selected values, or removable tokens.
avoidWhen: Avoid as a primary command button.
apiCaveats: Use tone for semantic/status color, variant for chip treatment such as soft, outline, solid, ghost, or dashed, and shape for rect or pill geometry. The default shape is rect.

preferWhen: Use to organize related PuChip items with consistent spacing and wrapping, or use fit mode for one-line metadata that should show only complete chips.
avoidWhen: Avoid for form fields requiring checkbox semantics unless the component exposes the needed behavior.
apiCaveats: fit mode measures rendered element children and hides overflow items; keep slot content element-based and avoid relying on hidden chip count.

preferWhen: Use for editing one chip value with chip geometry, commit/cancel, and optional remove.
avoidWhen: Avoid for chip collections with draft entry; use PuChipsEditor.
Avoid for passive status labels; use PuTag.
apiCaveats: Values are strings.
Enter emits commit; Escape restores the value captured when focus started.
Remove clears the value and emits remove.
Use PuChipsEditor for string arrays, draft entry, separators, and native repeated hidden inputs.
With surface-backed neutral variants, focused active border uses primary without changing the component tone.
Use shape rect or pill to match chip geometry; default is rect.

preferWhen: Use for collecting a string array as removable chips with draft text entry.
avoidWhen: Avoid for editing one chip value; use PuChipInput.
Avoid for passive status labels; use PuTag.
apiCaveats: Values are string arrays.
Press Enter or a configured separator to commit draft text.
Backspace removes the previous chip when draft text is empty.
With the default neutral tone and outline variant, focused active border uses primary without changing the component tone.
Use shape rect or pill to match chip geometry; default is rect.
Suggestions and custom option listbox behavior are deferred.

preferWhen: Use inside PuDescriptionList for one label-value pair.
avoidWhen: Avoid as a standalone layout primitive.
apiCaveats: PuDescriptionItem normalizes the parent list layout into only two internal layouts: stack for stack lists, and inline for inline or grid lists.

In stack item layout, suffix and action render in the label row even when no label text is present; in inline item layout, suffix or action is the definition value itself, not content appended after value.
Inline item layout keeps internal label/value columns across viewport changes; only the parent description-list grid may collapse its item columns.
Inline item layout defaults to start-aligned labels and end-aligned values; use valueAlign only when a value needs a different alignment.
Use padding only when an individual item should override the parent list density.
Use suffix for non-interactive value content such as badges or status markers; use the action slot when the value itself is an interactive affordance.

preferWhen: Use for grouped facts, profile details, order metadata, or read-only field summaries.
avoidWhen: Avoid for editable forms; use PuForm and field components.
apiCaveats: Use surfaceLevel for neutral page/content depth and variant for plain, soft, outline, or solid treatment.

preferWhen: Use for confirmation flows, destructive decisions, and short modal workflows that benefit from built-in title, description, icon, and actions structure.
avoidWhen: Avoid for fully custom overlay shells; use PuModal when the consumer should own most of the interior structure.
Avoid for secondary side-panel workflows; use PuDrawer.
apiCaveats: Uses required open plus close, cancel, and confirm events; do not assume v-model:open.
close emits a reason of overlay, escape, close, or cancel.
accessibilityNotes: Provide a useful title, title slot, or aria label for dialog context.
Use the actions slot when custom commands are needed while preserving footer spacing.

preferWhen: Use for secondary workflows, filters, or details that slide over the page.
avoidWhen: Avoid for blocking confirmation; use PuModal.
apiCaveats: Uses visible and update:visible for control; do not assume open or v-model:open.
Use header, title, close, default, and footer slots for composed drawer interiors; fullCustom is only for whole-interior escape hatches.
Use contentPadding=false when the slotted body owns its own edge-to-edge spacing.

preferWhen: Use when a region has no data, no search results, or needs a first-use prompt.
avoidWhen: Avoid for transient loading; use PuSkeleton.
apiCaveats: Use surfaceLevel for neutral page/content depth and variant for plain, soft, outline, or solid treatment.

preferWhen: Use for one attachment value by picker, drag/drop, or URL.
avoidWhen: Avoid for multi-file collections; use PuFilesUpload.

preferWhen: Use for collecting multiple files by picker, drag/drop, or URL.
avoidWhen: Avoid for a single attachment value; use PuFileUpload.

preferWhen: Use for persistent bottom panels whose height is changed by dragging a top handle and snapping between discrete stops.
avoidWhen: Avoid for modal or overlay workflows that need a scrim, focus trap, Escape close, or body scroll lock; use PuDrawer or PuModal instead.
apiCaveats: Uses modelValue and stops with numeric pixel heights; stop height is the whole panel height including the handle.
The panel has no mask, no Teleport, and no body scroll lock.
The content area scrolls independently; only the top handle starts dragging.
accessibilityNotes: Provide ariaLabel or ariaLabelledby for the handle slider.
ArrowUp and ArrowDown move between stops; Home and End jump to the lowest and highest stops.

preferWhen: Use as the structural container for grouped fields and submit actions.
avoidWhen: Avoid for read-only metadata; use PuDescriptionList.
apiCaveats: Native form attributes such as id, name, autocomplete, novalidate, action, and method fall through to the underlying form element.
PuForm prevents default native navigation and emits the native SubmitEvent; call validate() inside submit handlers when validation is needed.
External submit buttons can target a PuForm by passing the native form attribute with the form id.

preferWhen: Use to connect labels, helper text, required markers, errors, and any field control including custom controls.
avoidWhen: Avoid wrapping non-field display content.
apiCaveats: Explicit error text takes precedence over errors injected by PuForm prop matching.
Use the labelTrailing slot for field-level secondary affordances and the control slot when the default slot is not specific enough.

preferWhen: Use for package-consistent image rendering, loading placeholders, fallback initials, and avatar-like image surfaces.
avoidWhen: Avoid when native img with no package behavior is explicitly required.
apiCaveats: Use shape from the shared PuShape vocabulary instead of local radius values.
Use the fallback slot for custom fallback rendering; fallbackInitial and name cover simple initial fallback.
accessibilityNotes: Provide alt text for meaningful images; fallback content derives an accessible label from alt or name when available.

preferWhen: Use when users need to crop or adjust an uploaded image.
avoidWhen: Avoid for passive image display; use PuImg.

preferWhen: Use for local info, success, warning, or error messages inside a page section.
avoidWhen: Avoid for page-level announcement strips; use PuAnnouncementBar if it matches.

preferWhen: Use for page or region loading states that need a visible spinner with optional title and message.
avoidWhen: Avoid for inline control loading; use PuSpinner directly or PuButton loading state.
apiCaveats: The default surface is plain for page-level use; set surfaceLevel and variant when the loading state should be framed as a section.
accessibilityNotes: Uses status semantics and aria-busy while busy; provide message, title, or label so the loading state has useful accessible text.

preferWhen: Use for single-line string-backed text, search, URL, email, telephone, or date/time input with package field-control styling.
avoidWhen: Avoid for multi-line text; use PuTextarea.
Avoid for numeric application state; use PuNumberInput for number | null models.
apiCaveats: Use PuFormItem for labels, hints, and error messages.
Native input attributes such as id, name, autocomplete, list, form, and aria-* pass through to the underlying input; class and style apply to the component root.
Use list with a native datalist for free-text suggestions; use PuSelect only for fixed one-of-many choices.
Use update:modelValue as the live dirty hook and change for browser committed-value semantics.
Use size sm, md, or lg to map the control to caption, control, or body typography.
Use variant line, borderless, or outline to choose the field shell.
PuInput remains string-backed even when nativeType is number; use PuNumberInput for numeric app state.

preferWhen: Use for numeric application state that should bind as number | null.
avoidWhen: Avoid for string-backed numeric text that must preserve formatting exactly; use PuInput only when a string model is intentional.
apiCaveats: Empty input emits null.
Valid numeric input emits number values.
Invalid intermediate text is held locally and does not emit a misleading number.
Use min, max, and step for native numeric constraints.
Native input attributes such as id, name, autocomplete, form, and aria-* pass through to the underlying input; class and style apply to the component root.

preferWhen: Use for blocking decisions, confirmations, and focused short workflows.
avoidWhen: Avoid for secondary side-panel workflows; use PuDrawer.
apiCaveats: Uses required open plus close event; do not assume v-model:open.
accessibilityNotes: Provide a useful title or aria label for dialog context.

preferWhen: Use for a compact single-value control with a small ordered set of stops where the current label is shown elsewhere.
avoidWhen: Avoid for boolean settings; use PuToggleSwitch.
Avoid when all options need visible labels for comparison; use PuSegmented.
apiCaveats: Options provide accessible value text but are not rendered as visible labels.
Click, Enter, and Space advance using a bounce direction; arrow keys move linearly between stops.
accessibilityNotes: Provide ariaLabel or ariaLabelledby because the visual control only renders the rail and thumb.
The root uses slider semantics with aria-valuemin, aria-valuemax, aria-valuenow, and aria-valuetext.

preferWhen: Use for brief, non-blocking feedback that appears above the current page after an action.
avoidWhen: Avoid for persistent local validation or contextual messages; use PuInlineNotice inside the relevant section.
apiCaveats: Use duration 0 for persistent story or test examples; positive durations auto-dismiss.
Action clicks close the snackbar after emitting the action event.
accessibilityNotes: Warning and error tones use assertive live announcements; neutral, info, and success use polite announcements.

preferWhen: Use for application-managed snackbar queues with fixed positioning and max visible items.
avoidWhen: Avoid for a single inline notification embedded in page content; use PuInlineNotice.
apiCaveats: Uses items and update:items for queue control; do not assume an imperative toast service is included.

preferWhen: Use for prominent page-level or banner-style announcements.
avoidWhen: Avoid for local form helper text; use PuInlineNotice or PuFormItem.

preferWhen: Use for neutral page titles with optional subtitle, metadata row, and right-aligned actions.
avoidWhen: Avoid custom page header markup when this component covers the structure.
apiCaveats: PuPageHeader is neutral; it has no tone prop.
The structure is fixed to a main row and an optional meta row; there is no layout prop and no body/default slot.
Use the shared PuSize vocabulary for header scale; there is no density prop.
Use variant plain for no separator and variant line for a bottom separator.

preferWhen: Use for page-level structure across document pages, full-screen flows, centered flows, responsive aside layouts, and reveal-footer pages.
avoidWhen: Avoid for small local sections inside a page; use cards, sections, or normal layout utilities instead.
apiCaveats: Use padding with the shared PuSpacing vocabulary for scaffold-owned page inset control; padding none removes design padding and safe-area inset.

preferWhen: Use for selecting from option lists when a picker interaction is desired.
avoidWhen: Avoid for section navigation; use PuTabs.
apiCaveats: The web picker composes PuDrawer for its bottom overlay shell; keep picker-specific behavior in PuPicker and generic overlay behavior in PuDrawer.

preferWhen: Use for package-consistent scrollable content regions.
avoidWhen: Avoid adding nested scroll regions unless the layout requires them.

preferWhen: Use for compact single-choice mode, filter, or page-view switching with keyboard behavior.
avoidWhen: Avoid for large option sets or free-form navigation; use PuTabs, PuPicker, or application routing as appropriate.
apiCaveats: Use tone for semantic selected-item color and variant for rail treatment such as soft, outline, solid, ghost, or dashed.
accessibilityNotes: Use semantics="tabs" only when the items control associated tab panels.

preferWhen: Use inside PuSegmented for each selectable option.
avoidWhen: Avoid standalone usage because selection state and keyboard behavior come from PuSegmented.

preferWhen: Use for web-native one-of-many selection in product forms, settings, filters, or admin editors.
avoidWhen: Avoid for picker or drawer-style selection; use PuPicker when that interaction is desired.
Avoid for free text with suggestions; use PuInput list forwarding or a future autocomplete capability.
apiCaveats: Values are string | number | null.
Use placeholder plus clearable when null should remain selectable.
Native select attributes such as id, name, form, and aria-* pass through to the underlying select; class and style apply to the component root.
Use size sm, md, or lg for control scale; there is no density prop in the first API.
Multi-select, option groups, async loading, and custom option rendering are deferred.

preferWhen: Use while content is loading and the final layout shape is known.
avoidWhen: Avoid for empty data states; use PuEmptyState.

preferWhen: Use for indeterminate loading where the final layout shape is unknown or a skeleton would be too heavy.
avoidWhen: Avoid as a content placeholder when the final layout shape is known; use PuSkeleton.
apiCaveats: Color inherits currentColor; set a class or parent color for primary, muted, or context-specific spinner color instead of using a tone prop.
accessibilityNotes: Pass label when the spinner itself should announce loading; omit label when another region or control already exposes busy status.

preferWhen: Use inside PuTabs when individual tab components are required.
avoidWhen: Avoid standalone usage.
apiCaveats: Use the shared PuSize vocabulary for sizing.
Use variant line or pill to match the parent PuTabs presentation.
Use state dashed for pill tabs that represent expired or visually reduced options.

preferWhen: Use for switching between related sections where tab semantics are expected, including pill-styled horizontal tab bars.
avoidWhen: Avoid for picker-style option selection; use PuPicker or PuWheelPicker.
apiCaveats: Use value-based tabs with value and label fields; do not use index-only model values.
Use the shared PuSize vocabulary for sizing.
Use variant pill for transparent inactive tabs with primary-filled active state.
Use tabs[].state = "dashed" for expired or visually reduced pill tabs.
Use the append slot for trailing controls that are not part of the tablist.
accessibilityNotes: Disabled tabs are skipped by keyboard navigation.
ArrowLeft, ArrowRight, Home, and End move between enabled tabs.

preferWhen: Use for compact non-interactive status or category labels.
avoidWhen: Avoid for removable or selected tokens; use PuChip.
apiCaveats: Use tone for semantic/status color, variant for tag treatment, shape for rect or pill geometry, and size xs, sm, or md.

preferWhen: Use for multi-line text entry with package field-control styling.
avoidWhen: Avoid for single-line fields; use PuInput.
apiCaveats: Use PuFormItem for labels, hints, and error messages.
Native textarea attributes such as id, name, rows, form, and aria-* pass through to the underlying textarea; class and style apply to the component root.
Use rows for native low-risk sizing; autoHeight grows to content and disables manual resize while active.
Default manual resize is vertical.
Use update:modelValue as the live dirty hook and change for browser committed-value semantics.
Use size sm, md, or lg to map the control to caption, control, or body typography.
Use variant line, borderless, or outline to choose the field shell.
Focused border color follows tone; invalid overrides tone with the error color.

preferWhen: Use for immediate boolean settings or preferences.
avoidWhen: Avoid for deferred confirmation fields; use PuCheckbox.
apiCaveats: Use size sm, md, or lg to map the switch label to caption, control, or body typography.

preferWhen: Use for wheel-style option selection where that interaction matches the product surface.
avoidWhen: Avoid for section navigation; use PuTabs.
apiCaveats: Use tone for semantic highlight color and variant for picker surface treatment.
